rulings.Case Title: LELAND EVANS ET AL VS. DOMENIC J. CANNIZZARO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.S Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set Two, Number 15.1, Section (B) And (C)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 6, PLAINTIFF LELAND EVANS, JUAN REYES' Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.'s Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set Two, Number 15.1, Section (B) And (C). Plaintiff Leland Evans's motion to compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc. to provide further responses to Plaintiffs form interrogatories, set two, number 15.1, sections (b) and (c) and special interrogatories, set one number 19 within 10 days of notice of the Court's order, is granted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted in part. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant's responses are incomplete. Defendant has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. Defendant is obliged to obtain information from the property manager who is its agent and has not demonstrated that information in its property manager's possession is not within its possession, custody, or control. The court orders Defendant to provide an amended response to form interrogatories, set two, number 15.1. section (b) with complete list of witnesses who have knowledge of facts related to each of its affirmative defenses, and for section (c) to identify the documents supporting these affirmative defenses or clearly state it does not have knowledge of any documents supporting these defenses. Defendant shall also provide complete responses to special interrogatories, set one number 19 and give the names of the other tenants at the subject property who complained about Susan Anderson. Plaintiff's meet and confer was reasonable. Defendant must provide a verification. Further responses and verification are due no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. The court does not find Defendant acted with substantial justification. Defendant shall pay $3,000 to Plaintiff in sanctions. This reduced amount reflects the relative simplicity of the motion. Payment is due no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: LAURA KIRKHAM VS. PERSADO INC., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 1, DEFENDANT PERSADO INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. (Part 1 of 2). Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to counts one and five. A party may move for summary adjudication in an action or proceeding if it is contended that the cause of action has no merit. (Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(1).) The motion for summary adjudication shall be granted if all the papers submitted show that there is no triable issue as to any material fact. (Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(c).) The moving party must make an initial showing, based upon affirmative evidence, that summary adjudication must be granted. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Company (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) If the movant meets this initial burden, the burden then shifts to the opposing party to show that a triable issue of one or more material facts exists. The court liberally construes the evidence in support of the party opposing summary adjudication and resolves doubts concerning the evidence in favor of that party. (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1039.) The moving party bears the burden of persuasion "from the commencement to conclusion" to show "that there is no triable issue of material fact and that [it] is entitled to [summary adjudication] as a matter of law." (Ibid.) The court grants summary adjudication on the first cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff alleges that defendant "did not act in good faith with respect to its obligations under the Plan when it agreed to commercially unreasonable terms with respect to the Gap Deal, which had the effect of defeating Kirkham's reasonable compensation expectations under the Plan." (Complaint par. 20.) The complaint also apparently asserts that defendant's issuance of the Update to the Plan was done in bad faith. (Complaint, par. 9.) The claim lacks merit. Defendant had discretion to modify or update the Plan and the implied covenant cannot contradict an express term of the contract. (Undisputed Material Fact ("UMF") 14; Bevis v. Terrace View Partners, LP (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 230, 252 ["defendants here cannot be held liable for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing by implementing rent increases that the parties' written lease agreements expressly authorized."].) Moreover, even if the Update is unenforceable, the undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff did not comply with the Plan's terms to recover a commission. The Plan required a term of at least twelve months to recover. (Dean Decl., Ex B, p. 000032; Tamminga Depo., 12:4-8.) The Gap Deal had a seven-month term that was never increased to reach the threshold. (UMF 31, 40. Plaintiff purports to dispute UMF 31 but cites no evidence.) The Plan also permitted Defendant to adjust payable quotas downward where a booking was cancelled or a client did not pay (UMF 11); the Gap Deal never resulted in payment to Defendant (UMF 43). Plaintiff contends that the commission scheme was modified ("approved otherwise"), but the cited evidence refers to a vague purported addendum that fails to show that the express terms were in fact waived or modified. (Tamminga Depo., 40:22-41:1.) Plaintiff also fails to show that the implied covenant was breached because defendants' executives requested "commercially unreasonable terms." The implied covenant "cannot impose substantive duties or limits on the contracting parties beyond those incorporated in the specific terms of their agreement." (Guz v. Bechtel Nat. Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 317, 349-350.) Plaintiff does not sufficiently explain how the parties intended the Plan to somehow govern defendant's discretion to enter into customer agreements. (Continued to Part 2).

rulings.Case Title: LAURA KIRKHAM VS. PERSADO INC., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT PERSADO INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 1, DEFENDANT PERSADO INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. (Part 2 of 2). The court grants summary adjudication on the cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. First, defendant did not owe a commission and plaintiff therefore cannot show that the public policy to pay earned compensation was violated. Second, defendant presents evidence that plaintiff was discharged for performance issues. (Tamminga Decl., pars. 7-8.) Plaintiff does not show that this reason was pretextual. At most, plaintiff shows that she met some of Defendant's expectations while falling short in other areas. (Tamminga Decl., Ex. 3A1-A4.) The court overrules defendant's evidentiary objections. Defendant objects to plaintiff's separate statement responses, not the actual evidence. Defendant does not comply with CRC 3.1354(b) and specifically cite the objectionable evidence. There is also no basis for defendant to object to portions of plaintiff's points and authorities. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: BARRETT DAFFIN FRAPPIER TREDER & WEISS, LLP VS. KAYLA GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For 1 Statutory Discharge 2 Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Costs; And 3 Deposit Of Funds In Court; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BARRETT DAFFIN FRAPPIER TREDER & WEISS, LLP's Motion For 1. Statutory Discharge, 2. Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Costs; And 3. Deposit Of Funds In Court. The unopposed motion is granted. The court orders as follows: Plaintiff is a disinterested stakeholder, has fulfilled its duties under Civ. Code 2924j(c) and 2924k. Plaintiff shall recover its reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $2,137.50 and its costs in the amount of $1,295.52, for a total recovery of $3,433.02. Plaintiff currently holds $122,021.79 in surplus funds. It shall deposit these funds, less its recovery of $3,433.02, with the registry of this court. Upon doing so, Plaintiff shall be discharged from any further liability to the claimants, defendants in this interpleader matter. rulings.Case Title: GRUBMARKET, INC., VS. ABRAHAM FARMERS MARKET, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Abraham Farmers Market, Inc.S Answers To Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 8, PLAINTIFF GRUBMARKET, INC.'s, Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Abraham Farmers Market, Inc.'s Answers To Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Grubmarket Inc.'s motion to compel defendant Abraham Farmers Market Inc. to respond to its Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, is granted. Plaintiff failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2031.300(a).) Plaintiff is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents within 15 court days of entry of this order. Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court does not find substantial justification. Defendant shall remit sanctions in the amount of $667.20 to Plaintiff within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: GRUBMARKET, INC., VS. ABRAHAM FARMERS MARKET, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 8, PLAINTIFF GRUBMARKET, INC.'s, Motion For Order That Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Grubmarket, Inc.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. Defendant Abraham Farmers Market, Inc. failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by Defendant. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $866.60 payable by Defendant to Plaintiff. Defendant shall remit sanctions within 15 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. BUMBLE BEE FOODS, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 4, DEFENDANT BUMBLE BEE FOODS, LLC's Motion To Seal. The court exercises its discretion to hear this unopposed motion on shortened time. The court grants the motion to seal permanently the portions of the Supplemental Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Further Support of Motion to Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and the Declaration of Evan Smith in Further Support of the Motion to Approve Consent Judgment that were previously lodged provisionally under seal. The court finds that the material ot be sealed contains information proprietary to defendant that is maintained confidentially by defendant. The court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. BUMBLE BEE FOODS, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 4, PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4) provides: "If there is a settlement of an action brought by a person in the public interest under subdivision (d), the plaintiff shall submit the settlement, other than a voluntary dismissal in which no consideration is received from the defendant, to the court for approval upon noticed motion, and the court may approve the settlement only if the court makes all of the following findings: (A) The warning that is required by the settlement complies with this chapter. (B) The award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. (C) The penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in paragraph (2) of subdivision (b)." The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
